content_markdown: Changes in the climate are leading to a reduction in ice cover on Earth. Which of the following describes, for Earth, the change in albedo and the change in the rate of energy absorption?

diagram_metadata.description: A table with two columns: 'Change in albedo' and 'Change in rate of energy absorption'.
labels_to_preserve: Change in rate of energy absorption

options.label: A
options.text: Change in albedo: decrease; Change in rate of energy absorption: decrease

options.label: B
options.text: Change in albedo: decrease; Change in rate of energy absorption: increase

options.label: C
options.text: Change in albedo: increase; Change in rate of energy absorption: increase

options.label: D
options.text: Change in albedo: increase; Change in rate of energy absorption: decrease

vibe_explanation: Ice has a high albedo because it reflects a large portion of incoming solar radiation. If ice cover reduces, the Earth's surface becomes less reflective, meaning the albedo decreases. Since less energy is being reflected back into space, more energy must be absorbed by the Earth's surface, leading to an increase in the rate of energy absorption.
